data.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection). VERDICT: substantially a PASS — one MINOR coaching finding (missing-mechanism-detail / fitness gap), no critical/major.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria satisfied):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the dual-run parametrized harness is the canonical "verified fake" mechanism and is data-flow-complete against the actual code. The real client's enrichment surface and the existing fake expose IDENTICAL signatures — get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289) / get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391) / get_parent_map / search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) — and the existing FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py:30-62) returns fixture payloads verbatim with NO shape massaging, so a single parametrized contract body genuinely runs against both and "fake fails on divergence" is achievable.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the NAMED problem (fake-drift reintroducing bug-0ee6's fictional-shape failure), not a proxy. Shape/type assertions (keys + JSON primitive types, not exact values) directly target the nested-`comment` vs flat-`comments` / absent-`issuelinks` bug class; get_comment_map returns the raw nested {"comments":[...], "total":N} field (acli_graph.py:303-308) — exactly the 0ee6 seam.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present (G6 item 4): the "Why" states an explicit, grounded rationale (verified-fake pattern from SWE@Google; prevents 0ee6 drift).
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) one-directional snapshot/golden assertion of the real client's shape with no shared test body, and (b) pure OpenAPI-schema validation of real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) never exercises the fake under the same assertions so it cannot catch fake drift (the named problem); (b) depends on Jira Cloud spec completeness (an open question the plan itself flags) and still wouldn't pin the FAKE. The chosen shared-contract-body approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on testability and robustness. Alternatives discarded — no finding.

COACHING FINDING (minor — fitness-for-purpose gap in the mechanism's DEFAULT-suite leg):
- [ ] Make the "fake fails on divergence" mechanism non-vacuous in the default (hermetic) suite: name HOW the real-client shape is available to the assertion when the live leg is OFF (the default).
  - criteria: [G6]
  - location: "What" bullet 3 ("The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") and Acceptance Criteria line 2.
  - evidence: ["plan AC2: 'The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.'", "real client runs ONLY behind the opt-in live/external marker per 'What' bullet 2 and AC1/AC4 — so in the default suite there is no live real-shape to diverge against, making AC2 enforceable only on the rare opt-in live run and weakening fitness-for-purpose", "tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py returns fixtures captured FROM the real client verbatim (NO shape massaging) — the hermetic divergence signal already exists, but the plan does not name the fixture-derived shape as the default-suite enforcement point"]
  - suggested_fix: State that the contract assertions run against the fake's fixture-derived output in the DEFAULT hermetic suite (where divergence surfaces immediately, since the fixtures are real captured payloads), and frame the live-gated leg as an ADDITIONAL freshness check that the fixtures still match live Jira — not the sole place AC2 is enforced.

(Note: the "Optional OpenAPI validation" bullet's external-feasibility concern is a T3 matter and is already tracked in this ticket's T3 audits; not re-filed under G6.)
